query: Boeing Q4S mission entanglement swapping lab test payload compact satellite specification technical details

mako.raw:
{"query":"Boeing Q4S mission entanglement swapping lab test payload compact satellite specification technical details","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1639,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.boeing.com/features/2026/06/boeing-takes-a-major-step-toward-the-quantum-internet","title":"Boeing takes a major step toward the quantum internet","content":"Boeing team members inspect the Q4S flight payload during testing designed to verify the hardware can withstand launch and on-orbit conditions ahead of its planned 2027 mission.
Boeing has reached a major milestone in its effort to help build a future global quantum internet, successfully demonstrating a key quantum networking capability on compact, space-qualified flight hardware and moving the Q4S mission into final spacecraft integration.
The team demonstrated high-fidelity entanglement swapping during ground testing on a compact, space-qualified payload, then completed environmental qualification testing and delivered the flight hardware for final spacecraft integration.
What’s next: As Boeing moves Q4S toward launch, the team expects the mission to generate data that helps shape future quantum networking architectures and informs follow-on systems."},{"url":"https://thequantuminsider.com/2026/06/18/boeing-lab-tests-mark-major-step-toward-space-based-quantum-network/","title":"Boeing Lab Tests Mark Major Step Toward Space-Based Quantum Network","content":"Boeing reported that its Q4S satellite payload successfully demonstrated high-fidelity entanglement swapping during ground testing, marking a step toward space-based quantum networking.
The compact, space-qualified payload also completed environmental qualification testing and is undergoing final spacecraft integration ahead of a planned 2027 launch and one-year on-orbit demonstration.
Boeing said the mission is intended to validate quantum networking technologies that could support future secure communications, precision timing, distributed sensing and a global quantum internet.
This result marks a major step toward proving quantum networking in orbit.
The team also completed environmental qualification testing, a series of tests designed to verify that the flight payload can withstand launch stresses and the harsh conditions of space.
Q4S is a key step in moving quantum networking toward practical aerospace and defense applications."},{"url":"https://quantumcomputingreport.com/boeing-advances-its-space-based-quantum-networking-project-with-successful-q4s-lab-tests/","title":"Boeing Advances Its Space-Based Quantum Networking Project with Successful Q4S Lab Tests - Quantum Computing Report","content":"Q4S is a planned one-year, on-orbit demonstration mission designed to test quantum networking hardware in the harsh environment of space.
These test results show that we can produce high-fidelity swaps on a payload engineered for space, not just for a controlled lab bench.
Following its 2027 launch, Boeing intends to collect one year of on-orbit performance data and submit the technical results for peer-reviewed publication."},{"url":"https://spacenews.com/boeing-demonstrates-quantum-protocol-in-payload-set-for-2027-launch/","title":"Boeing demonstrates quantum protocol in payload set for 2027 launch - SpaceNews","content":"Home / Boeing demonstrates quantum protocol in payload set for 2027 launch
PORTLAND, Ore. – Boeing demonstrated a key quantum networking protocol in ground testing of a compact payload ahead of on-orbit experiment in 2027.
“High-fidelity entanglement swapping” was demonstrated earlier this year by Boeing’s Q4S quantum networking satellite system, Boeing announced June 18.
Q4S, slated to conduct a one-year demonstration in orbit, to advance the company’s “long-term vision to enable a global quantum internet that connects quantum sensors and computing systems across vast distances,” according to the news release."}]}
